etymology.description: semantic 羽(羽) (yǔ, "wings") + phonetic 公(公) (gōng) -> originally meant "feathers" -> loaned to "old man" through association with 公
etymology.interpretation: Starting with the original meaning of "feathers," the character was borrowed for its sound to convey the concept of an "old man" due to the phonetic presence of 公(公) (gōng, "elder; esteemed man"). Over time, the connection to "feathers" became obsolete, while the association with age and wisdom remained.

examples.chinese: 那位老翁在树下休息(那位老翁在樹下休息)。
examples.pinyin: Nà wèi lǎowēng zài shù xià xiūxi.
examples.english: That old man is resting under the tree.

examples.chinese: 村里的长者们都尊敬这位智翁(村裡的長者們都尊敬這位智翁)。
examples.pinyin: Cūn lǐ de zhǎngzhěmen dōu zūnjìng zhè wèi zhìwēng.
examples.english: The village elders all respect this wise old man.

examples.chinese: 他是一位有声望的长翁(他是一位有聲望的長翁)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì yī wèi yǒu shēngwàng de chángwēng.
examples.english: He is a highly respected elder.
